crags.name: Cedar Creek
crags.subtitle: Mt Nebo / D'Aguilar Range
crags.description: Cedar Creek is one of Southeast Queensland's finest bouldering areas, featuring high-quality rhyolite on the eastern escarpment of the D'Aguilar Range. Following sustained engagement with Brisbane City Council, bouldering has been formally recognised as a legitimate recreational activity within the park — backed by the installation of access stairs and official signage.

crags.name: White Rock
crags.subtitle: White Rock Conservation Park, Ipswich
crags.description: White Rock is one of SEQLD's most significant but underdocumented climbing areas, featuring distinctive pale rhyolite in a conservation park setting. ACC is working closely with a dedicated Ipswich City Council contact toward formal recognition of bouldering — building on the precedent set at Cedar Creek.

crags.name: Tooheys Forest
crags.subtitle: Tooheys Forest, Tarragindi · Brisbane
crags.description: Tooheys Forest offers accessible urban bouldering within Brisbane's southern suburbs — a rare find this close to the city. The area contains quality sandstone problems across a range of grades. ACC is currently in the research and access assessment phase for this crag.

crags.name: Plunketts
crags.subtitle: Samford Valley · Brisbane
crags.description: Plunketts is a quality bouldering area in the Samford Valley with a loyal local following. The area features a range of styles across multiple rock types. ACC has identified this as a priority crag for documentation once White Rock engagement is progressed.
